examples.title: Simple Waterfall Process Flow
examples.description: Demonstrates sequential phase execution in Waterfall model
examples.code:
PROCEDURE WaterfallDevelopment
BEGIN
 Phase1: RequirementsAnalysis
 gatherUserRequirements()
 createSRS()
 IF SRSApproved THEN
 Phase2: SystemDesign
 
 Phase2: SystemDesign
 createArchitecturalDesign()
 createDetailedDesign()
 IF DesignApproved THEN
 Phase3: Implementation

 Phase3: Implementation
 writeCode()
 unitTesting()
 IF CodeComplete THEN
 Phase4: Testing

 Phase4: Testing
 integrationTesting()
 systemTesting()
 acceptanceTesting()
 IF AllTestsPass THEN
 Phase5: Deployment

 Phase5: Deployment
 installSoftware()
 userTraining()
 Phase6: Maintenance

 Phase6: Maintenance
 bugFixes()
 updates()
 enhancements()
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows how Waterfall follows strict sequential phases where each phase must complete before the next begins. Decision points check for approval before moving forward.

examples.title: Spiral Model Iteration
examples.description: Shows one iteration cycle of the Spiral Model
examples.code:
PROCEDURE SpiralIteration(currentPhase, riskLevel)
BEGIN
 WHILE productNotComplete DO
 // Planning Phase
 determineObjectives()
 identifyConstraints()
 planNextDeliverable()
 
 // Risk Analysis Phase
 identifyRisks()
 analyzeRiskSeverity()
 IF riskLevel > threshold THEN
 developPrototype()
 evaluatePrototype()
 updateRiskLevel()
 ENDIF
 
 // Engineering Phase
 developComponent()
 integrateWithPrevious()
 testComponent()
 
 // Evaluation Phase
 evaluateResults()
 getCustomerFeedback()
 updateRequirements()
 
 // Move to next spiral
 currentPhase = currentPhase + 1
 ENDWHILE
END
examples.explanation: Each spiral iteration includes planning, risk analysis, engineering, and evaluation. The loop continues until the complete product is delivered, with risk analysis happening at each iteration.
examples.timeComplexity: O(k*n) where k is number of spiral iterations
examples.spaceComplexity: O(m) where m is number of risk factors tracked

examples.title: Requirements Elicitation Process
examples.description: Shows steps in gathering requirements from stakeholders
examples.code:
PROCEDURE RequirementsElicitation
BEGIN
 stakeholders = identifyStakeholders()
 techniques = [Interview, Survey, Observation, Workshop]
 
 FOR EACH technique IN techniques
 FOR EACH stakeholder IN stakeholders
 data = applyTechnique(technique, stakeholder)
 rawRequirements = rawRequirements + data
 ENDFOR
 ENDFOR
 
 // Analysis and Consolidation
 requirements = analyzeRequirements(rawRequirements)
 removeConflicts(requirements)
 prioritizeRequirements(requirements)
 
 // Documentation
 SRS = documentRequirements(requirements)
 reviewSRS()
 validateWithStakeholders()
 
 RETURN SRS
END
examples.explanation: This demonstrates the requirements elicitation sub-process of Requirements Engineering, showing how data is collected from multiple sources using various techniques and consolidated into an SRS document.
